svg.title: SMTP Response Code Reference
svg.desc: Curated reference of SMTP response codes most relevant to email deliverability and sender compliance. Covers RFC 5321 standard codes and provider-specific enforcement codes from Gmail, Microsoft, and Yahoo introduced with the 2024–2025 bulk sender requirements. Gmail enforcement codes are shown as paired warning (4xx) to rejection (5xx) escalations: the same compliance issue starts as a temporary rate limit and escalates to permanent rejection if not fixed. This is not an exhaustive protocol reference — for the complete specification see IETF RFC 5321.

cc:Work.dc:title: SMTP Response Code Reference for Email Deliverability
cc:Work.dc:creator: InboxAlly
cc:Work.dc:subject: SMTP codes, SMTP error codes, bounce codes, email deliverability, Gmail error codes, Yahoo error codes, Microsoft error codes, 550 5.7.26, 550 5.7.25, 550 5.7.27, 550 5.7.28, 550 5.7.29, 550 5.7.30, 550 5.7.515, 421 4.7.26, 421 4.7.27, 421 4.7.28, 421 4.7.29, 421 4.7.30, 421 4.7.32, 421 4.7.23, 553 5.7.1, 554 5.7.9, 421 4.7.0, sender requirements, bulk sender, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, TLS, PTR, RFC 5321
cc:Work.dc:description: Practitioner-facing SMTP code lookup organized by severity tier with paired Gmail warning-to-rejection escalation mapping and cross-provider enforcement codes from Google, Microsoft, and Yahoo.

svg.text: Codes most relevant to email deliverability and sender compliance — not an exhaustive protocol reference

g.aria-label: How to read SMTP codes: three-digit code structure and enhanced code format
g.data-description: Visual breakdown of the three-digit SMTP code system. First digit is severity (2=success, 3=intermediate, 4=temporary failure, 5=permanent failure), second digit is category (x.7.x=security/policy, x.1.x=addressing, x.2.x=mailbox), third digit is specific detail. Enhanced codes like 550 5.7.26 follow base-reply plus enhanced-status format.
g.text: Enhanced codes (e.g., 550 5.7.26 = base reply + enhanced status):
class.subject.detail — x.7.x = security/policy · x.1.x = addressing · x.2.x = mailbox

g.aria-label: 2xx Success codes: service ready, closing, auth success, OK, will forward, will attempt
g.data-description: Six standard 2xx success codes from RFC 5321: 220 service ready, 221 closing, 235 auth successful, 250 OK, 251 will forward, 252 will attempt delivery.
g.text: Cannot verify user — will attempt delivery

g.aria-label: 3xx Intermediate codes: authentication challenge and start mail input
g.data-description: Two standard 3xx intermediate codes from RFC 5321: 334 server authentication challenge, 354 start mail input.
g.text: Start mail input — send message content

g.aria-label: 4xx Temporary failure codes: standard RFC 5321 codes where the sending server retries automatically
g.data-description: Five standard 4xx temporary failure codes from RFC 5321: 421 service unavailable, 450 mailbox busy, 451 local error, 452 insufficient storage, 455 can't accommodate parameters. Sending server retries automatically.
g.text: Your server retries automatically — fix the issue before providers escalate to 5xx
Mailbox unavailable — busy or temp policy block
Recipient server temp rejecting — retry later

g.aria-label: 5xx Permanent failure codes: standard RFC 5321 codes where the message is permanently rejected
g.data-description: Twelve standard 5xx permanent failure codes from RFC 5321 and RFC 7504: 500 syntax error, 501 parameter syntax, 502 not implemented, 503 bad sequence, 521 server does not accept mail, 550 mailbox unavailable, 551 user not local, 552 exceeded storage, 553 mailbox name not allowed, 554 transaction failed, 556 domain does not accept mail.
g.text: Message rejected — fix the compliance issue so future messages from your domain are accepted
Host blocks all SMTP — find alternate contact
Mailbox unavailable — not found or policy block
Verify address or check sender policy
Domain rejects all mail — find alternate contact

g.aria-label: Bulk sender enforcement codes from Gmail and Microsoft, 2024–2025, shown as paired warning to rejection escalations
g.data-description: Gmail enforcement codes shown as paired 4xx warning to 5xx rejection escalations. Warning codes ride inside 421 base reply, rejection codes inside 550 base reply. Covers: 421 4.7.26/550 5.7.26 (unauthenticated), 421 4.7.27/550 5.7.27 (SPF failure), 421 4.7.28/550 5.7.28 (spam rate), 421 4.7.29/550 5.7.29 (no TLS), 421 4.7.30/550 5.7.30 (DKIM failure), 421 4.7.32/550 5.7.26 (no DMARC alignment), 421 4.7.23/550 5.7.25 (no PTR record).
g.text: Same issue, two severity levels — 4xx warns, 5xx rejects
No PTR record / reverse DNS mismatch
* No documented 550 5.7.32 — alignment failures escalate to 550 5.7.26 (unauthenticated) or 550 5.7.1 (general block)

g.aria-label: Gmail permanent rejection codes with no 4xx warning equivalent — these go straight to rejection
g.data-description: Two Gmail enforcement codes with no 4xx warning equivalent: 550 5.7.1 (blocked for spam, low reputation, DMARC policy, or invalid headers) and 550 5.7.23 (DMARC policy failure with quarantine or reject applied). These go straight to permanent rejection.
g.text: GMAIL — PERMANENT REJECTION ONLY (NO 421 WARNING EQUIVALENT)
Blocked — spam, low reputation, DMARC policy, or invalid From: header
DMARC policy failure (quarantine or reject applied)
Fix DMARC record, check SPF + DKIM alignment

g.aria-label: Microsoft Outlook enforcement code: 550 5.7.515 required authentication level not met
g.data-description: Microsoft's single enforcement code: 550 5.7.515 — required authentication level not met, requiring SPF + DKIM + DMARC (p=none minimum). Active since May 2025.
g.text: Required authentication level not met (SPF + DKIM + DMARC)

g.aria-label: Yahoo and AOL enforcement codes: temporary deferrals, Spamhaus blocks, permanent deferrals, and policy rejections
g.data-description: Four Yahoo/AOL enforcement codes active since February 2024: 421 4.7.0 [TSS04] (temporary deferral for unusual volume or complaints), 553 5.7.1 [BL21/BL23] (IP blocked on Spamhaus SBL or XBL), 553 5.7.2 [TSS11] (permanent deferral — retrying will not succeed), 554 5.7.9 (policy rejection for unauthenticated or content violation). Sourced from senders.yahooinc.com/smtp-error-codes.
g.text: Temporarily deferred — unusual volume or user complaints
IP blocked — listed on Spamhaus (SBL or XBL)
All messages permanently deferred — retrying will NOT succeed
Policy rejection — unauthenticated or content violation

g.aria-label: Common recipient-side codes that senders cannot fix — these are the recipient's problem
g.data-description: Seven codes the sender sees but cannot fix: 450 4.2.1 (rate limited), 452 4.2.2 (over quota temp), 550 5.1.1 (account doesn't exist), 550 5.2.1 (account disabled), 552 5.2.2 (over quota permanent), 552 5.2.3 (message too large), 552 5.7.0 (illegal attachment).
g.text: COMMON RECIPIENT-SIDE CODES (SENDER CANNOT FIX — RECIPIENT ISSUE)
Recipient receiving too fast — rate limited

g.aria-label: Quick reference: 4xx temporary failure means server retries automatically, 5xx permanent failure means message is rejected
g.data-description: Summary boxes: 4xx is a temporary failure where your server retries automatically — fix the issue before providers escalate to 5xx. 5xx is a permanent failure where the message is rejected and your server stops trying.
g.text: This is a warning — fix the issue before providers escalate to permanent 5xx rejection.
This message is permanently rejected. Your server stops trying.
Fix the compliance issue so future messages from your domain are accepted.
Since November 2025, Gmail converts 421 warnings to 550 rejections for persistent non-compliance
A 421 4.7.x code today becomes a 550 5.7.x rejection tomorrow if you don't fix the issue

svg.text: This reference covers codes most relevant to email deliverability and sender compliance.
For the complete SMTP protocol specification, see

g.aria-label: Sources: IETF RFC 5321, RFC 7504, RFC 3463, Google Workspace Admin Help, Microsoft Tech Community, Yahoo Sender Hub
g.data-description: Standard codes from IETF RFC 5321 (Oct 2008), RFC 7504 (Jun 2015), RFC 3463. Gmail enforcement codes from Google Workspace Admin Help. Microsoft code from Microsoft Tech Community (Apr 2025). Yahoo codes from senders.yahooinc.com/smtp-error-codes.
